channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump fills his government with billionaires after running on a working-class message
item.description: President Donald Trump's second White House is looking a lot like the inside of Mar-a-Lago, with extremely wealthy Americans taking key roles in his Republican administration.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US places dozens of senior aid officials on leave, citing possible resistance to Trump orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At least 56 senior officials in the top U.S. aid and development agency were placed on leave Monday amid an investigation into an alleged effort to thwart President Donald Trump’s orders. A current official and a former official at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Comer launches investigation of sanctuary cities, asks mayors to testify
item.description: U.S. James Comer is asking sanctuary mayors to testify before the House Committee on Oversight and Reform on their sanctuary city policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada Republicans pass Democrats in voter registration, PAC reports
item.description: Nevada Republicans said Monday they have passed Democrats in voter registration in the state for the first time since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favo
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favorable to the presi
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Duffy nomination to run Transportation Department
item.description: Former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) moved a step closer to becoming the next Transportation secretary as the Senate voted unanimously to tee up a final vote to confirm him. Senators voted 97-0 to invoke cloture on Duffy’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Bessent as Trump's Treasury secretary
item.description: Scott Bessent on Monday secured confirmation to lead the Department of the Treasury, putting President Trump’s top economic nominee in place ahead of a daunting GOP push to enact Trump's ambitious economic agenda. Senators voted 68-29 on Bessent’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State AGs warn retail giant Costco for doubling down on 'discriminatory' DEI
item.description: State AGs in 19 states urge Costco to end discriminatory DEI policies, aligning with Trump’s executive order to enforce civil rights laws and investigate violators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court leaves in place Mississippi’s voting ban for people convicted of some crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday left in place Mississippi’s Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance political advisers join Ramaswamy ahead of expected Ohio governor run
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy is set to launch his bid mid-February, per the source familiar, to succeed term-limited Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: New Trump orders on transgender troops, COVID and more expected on Hegseth’s first day
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will sign a flurry of executive orders focused on the military, including to reinstate troops booted for refusing COVID-19 vaccines and remove “transgender ideology” from the force.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers hit a reset after Supreme Court ruling, but stalemate persists
item.description: Like a scene from the movie “Groundhog Day,” life in the Minnesota House continued to repeat itself Monday — a cycle of gaveled to order, no quorum, no business, gaveled to adjournment. The Senate also saw some controversy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Israel and Lebanon ceasefire extended: White House
item.description: The ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel, announced by former President Biden in November, is now set “to be in effect until February 18, 2025,” the statement reads.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump reverses Biden's ban on private prison contracts
item.description: Trump's action comes after signals from private prison corporations like GEO Group and CoreCivic that they expected such a reversal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell opposes Hegseth on final confirmation vote
item.description: His vote came as a surprise after he voted Thursday night to advance Hegseth’s nomination to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota justices tell lawmakers to work out control of ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court on Friday agreed with DFLers that it takes 68 votes to conduct House business, but the opinion didn’t come with a road map on how the House should proceed from here.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants to pull US out of World Health Organization again; here's what may happen next
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
